name: British Museum
description: World-famous collection of human history and culture, from the Rosetta Stone to the Parthenon sculptures.

name: National Gallery
description: National collection of Western European painting from the 13th to the 19th century, on Trafalgar Square.

name: Tate Modern
description: Britain's national museum of modern and contemporary art, in a former Bankside power station.

name: Tate Britain
description: Houses the national collection of British art from 1500 to the present, including the Turner Bequest.

name: Natural History Museum
description: Vast natural-world collection spanning dinosaurs, the blue whale and the wonders of the planet.

name: Science Museum
description: Science, technology and engineering from the Industrial Revolution to space exploration.

name: Victoria and Albert Museum
description: The world's leading museum of art, design and performance.

name: National Portrait Gallery
description: Portraits of the most famous and significant people in British history, recently refurbished.

name: Wallace Collection
description: A national collection of fine and decorative arts in a historic London townhouse, free to all.

name: Imperial War Museum London
description: Explores modern conflict and its impact on people’s lives, from WWI to the present.

name: National Army Museum
description: The story of the British Army and its role in society, in Chelsea.

name: National Maritime Museum
description: The world's largest maritime museum, in Greenwich.

name: Queen's House
description: Inigo Jones’s pioneering classical villa in Greenwich, home to a fine art collection.

name: Wellcome Collection
description: Free museum and library exploring health, medicine and what it means to be human.

name: British Library
description: One of the world's great research libraries; its Treasures Gallery shows Magna Carta and more.
note: Treasures Gallery free; some exhibitions paid.

name: Sir John Soane's Museum
description: The architect's atmospheric house-museum, preserved as it was at his death in 1837.

name: Young V&A
description: The V&A's museum of childhood and creativity for young people, in Bethnal Green.

name: Horniman Museum & Gardens
description: Anthropology, natural history and music collections set in 16 acres of gardens.

name: Guildhall Art Gallery
description: The City of London's art gallery, with a preserved Roman amphitheatre beneath it.

name: Museum of the Home
description: Explores the way we live through recreated period rooms and gardens, in former almshouses.

name: Bank of England Museum
description: The history of the Bank of England, where you can lift a real gold bar.

name: London Museum Docklands
description: The history of London's river, port and Docklands, in a Georgian warehouse.

name: V&A East Museum
description: New V&A museum in Stratford showcasing creativity and design, opened in 2026.

name: V&A East Storehouse
description: The V&A's working collection store in Stratford, offering behind-the-scenes access.

name: Tower of London
description: Historic royal fortress and palace, home to the Crown Jewels and the Yeoman Warders.

name: Hampton Court Palace
description: Henry VIII's spectacular Tudor palace with Baroque additions and famous gardens.

name: Kensington Palace
description: Working royal residence and museum in Kensington Gardens, birthplace of Queen Victoria.

name: Churchill War Rooms
description: Churchill's secret underground WWII command centre, preserved beneath Whitehall.

name: HMS Belfast
description: A WWII Royal Navy cruiser preserved as a floating museum on the Thames.

name: Cutty Sark
description: The world’s sole surviving tea clipper, in dry dock at Greenwich.
note: ~£22 adult; RMG Day Pass £38 with Observatory.

name: Royal Observatory Greenwich
price_text: £24 adult; £38 day pass with Cutty Sark
description: Home of Greenwich Mean Time and the Prime Meridian, with astronomy galleries.

name: Westminster Abbey
description: Gothic abbey and royal church, site of coronations and burials for over 1,000 years.
opening_hours: Mon–Sat (times vary; closed to tourists Sun)

name: Apsley House (Wellington Museum)
description: The Duke of Wellington's grand London home, with a fine art collection.

name: Kenwood House
description: Neoclassical villa on Hampstead Heath with an exceptional collection of paintings, free to enter.

name: Eltham Palace
description: Medieval royal palace fused with a spectacular Art Deco mansion.

name: Down House
description: The home of Charles Darwin, where he wrote On the Origin of Species.

name: Marble Hill House
description: Palladian villa beside the Thames in Richmond, built for a royal mistress.

name: Ranger's House (Wernher Collection)
description: Georgian villa in Greenwich housing the Wernher Collection of art.

name: Jewel Tower
description: A surviving 14th-century tower of the medieval Palace of Westminster.

name: Wellington Arch
description: Triumphal arch at Hyde Park Corner with galleries and a viewing platform.

name: 2 Willow Road
description: Ernö Goldfinger's modernist Hampstead home, in the care of the National Trust.

name: Carlyle's House
description: The Chelsea home of writer Thomas Carlyle, preserved as a Victorian interior.

name: Fenton House
description: 17th-century merchant’s house in Hampstead with a walled garden and keyboard instruments.

name: Ham House
description: A rare survival of 17th-century Stuart taste and fashion, beside the Thames.

name: Osterley Park and House
description: Grand Robert Adam mansion and landscaped park in west London.

name: Sutton House
description: A rare Tudor house in Hackney, built in 1535.

name: Red House
description: William Morris's Arts and Crafts home, designed with Philip Webb.

name: Eastbury Manor House
description: A well-preserved Elizabethan manor house in Barking.

name: Rainham Hall
description: 18th-century Georgian house with a garden and café, in Havering.

name: Charles Dickens Museum
description: Dickens's only surviving London home, where he wrote Oliver Twist.

name: Freud Museum
description: The final home of Sigmund Freud, complete with his famous psychoanalytic couch.

name: Keats House
description: The Hampstead home where Romantic poet John Keats wrote some of his finest verse.

name: Dr Johnson's House
description: The Georgian townhouse where Samuel Johnson compiled his Dictionary.

name: Handel Hendrix House
description: The adjoining Mayfair homes of composer Handel and rock guitarist Jimi Hendrix.

name: Leighton House Museum
description: The opulent studio-house of Victorian artist Frederic, Lord Leighton.

name: 18 Stafford Terrace (Linley Sambourne House)
description: A perfectly preserved Victorian middle-class home in Kensington.

name: Hogarth's House
description: The country home of painter William Hogarth, in Chiswick.

name: Dennis Severs' House
description: An immersive, candlelit "still-life drama" recreating 18th-century life in Spitalfields.

name: Benjamin Franklin House
description: The world's only surviving home of Benjamin Franklin, near Trafalgar Square.

name: Spencer House
description: London's finest surviving 18th-century aristocratic palace, built for the Spencer family.

name: Strawberry Hill House
description: Horace Walpole's flamboyant Gothic Revival villa in Twickenham.

name: Chiswick House
description: A perfect Palladian villa with 18th-century landscaped gardens.

name: Pitzhanger Manor
description: The country home Sir John Soane designed for himself, with an attached gallery.

name: Fulham Palace
description: Former country home of the Bishops of London, with a museum, gardens and café.

name: Valentines Mansion
description: A Grade II* Georgian mansion with gardens in Ilford.

name: Southside House
description: An eccentric 17th-century house on Wimbledon Common, seen by guided tour.

name: Postal Museum
description: Five centuries of postal history, plus a ride on the underground Mail Rail.
note: ~£18.50 adult incl. Mail Rail; annual ticket.

name: London Transport Museum
description: The story of London's transport, with historic buses, trams and Tube trains.

name: Design Museum
description: Dedicated to contemporary product, industrial, graphic and fashion design.

name: Fashion and Textile Museum
description: Changing exhibitions of fashion, textiles and jewellery in Bermondsey.

name: Cartoon Museum
description: British cartoons, comics and caricature from the 18th century to today.

name: Foundling Museum
description: Tells the story of the Foundling Hospital, Britain's first children's charity, with Hogarth and Handel.

name: Garden Museum
description: Britain's museum of the history and design of gardens, in a former Lambeth church.

name: Estorick Collection of Modern Italian Art
description: Modern Italian art, including Futurism, in a Georgian house in Islington.

name: Dulwich Picture Gallery
description: England's first purpose-built public art gallery, designed by Sir John Soane.
note: £10 adult; under-18s free. England's oldest public gallery.

name: Fan Museum
description: The world's only museum devoted entirely to fans and the art of fan-making.

name: Brunel Museum
description: Celebrates the engineering of the Brunels and the Thames Tunnel, in Rotherhithe.

name: Florence Nightingale Museum
description: The life and work of the founder of modern nursing, by St Thomas' Hospital.
opening_hours: Tue–Sun 10:00–17:00 (last entry 16:30); last Thu monthly until 20:00

name: Sherlock Holmes Museum
description: A recreation of the fictional detective's lodgings at 221b Baker Street.

name: Pollock's Toy Museum
description: Antique toys, dolls and toy theatres; currently operating as pop-ups.
note: Fitzrovia site closed Jan 2023; no permanent home.

name: Vagina Museum
description: The world's first bricks-and-mortar museum dedicated to gynaecological anatomy and health.

name: Museum of Brands
description: The story of consumer culture through packaging and advertising, in Notting Hill.

name: Hunterian Museum
address: Royal College of Surgeons, Lincoln's Inn Fields
description: Anatomical and surgical specimens from the collection of surgeon John Hunter.

name: Old Operating Theatre Museum & Herb Garret
description: Europe's oldest surviving surgical operating theatre, in a church garret.

name: Clink Prison Museum
description: On the site of one of England's oldest prisons, on Bankside.

name: London Canal Museum
description: London's canals and the people who lived and worked on them, by a Victorian ice well.

name: Magic Circle Museum
description: The history and secrets of magic, home to The Magic Circle (events/appointment only).

name: Twinings Museum
description: A small museum on the history of tea inside Twinings' 300-year-old shop on the Strand.

name: Viktor Wynd Museum of Curiosities
description: An eccentric cabinet of curiosities, art and natural history in Hackney.

name: Bow Street Police Museum
description: The history of the Bow Street Runners and the Metropolitan Police, in a former police station.

name: City of London Police Museum
description: The history of the City of London Police, at the Guildhall.

name: London Museum of Water & Steam
description: Working steam pumping engines telling the story of London’s water supply.

name: Crossness Pumping Station
description: A magnificent Victorian sewage pumping station with ornate ironwork, open on event days.

name: Musical Museum
description: A collection of self-playing automatic musical instruments, in Brentford.

name: Jewish Museum London
description: British Jewish history and culture; the collection is currently in transition.

name: Black Cultural Archives
description: The national home of Black British history, in Brixton.

name: Wiener Holocaust Library
description: The world's oldest Holocaust archive and a leading research library.

name: Cinema Museum
description: Cinema history and memorabilia in a former workhouse where Charlie Chaplin once stayed.

name: Petrie Museum of Egyptian & Sudanese Archaeology
description: One of the world’s great collections of Egyptian and Sudanese archaeology, at UCL.

name: Grant Museum of Zoology
description: London's only remaining university zoological museum, at UCL.

name: UCL Art Museum
description: UCL's collection of art, including works by Slade School students and masters.

name: Courtauld Gallery
description: Renowned collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces at Somerset House.

name: RCM Museum of Music
description: Historic musical instruments at the Royal College of Music.

name: Royal Academy of Music Museum
description: Instruments and music history at Britain’s oldest conservatoire.

name: Gordon Museum of Pathology
description: The UK's largest pathology museum, open to medical professionals only.

name: Royal College of Physicians Museum
description: Medical history and rare books in a striking modernist building by Regent’s Park.

name: Old Speech Room Gallery (Harrow School)
description: Antiquities and art at Harrow School, open on a limited basis.

name: Royal Air Force Museum London
description: The story of the RAF and aviation, with historic aircraft, in Colindale.

name: Household Cavalry Museum
description: The working stables and history of the Household Cavalry, on Horse Guards.

name: Guards Museum
description: The history of the five regiments of Foot Guards, near Buckingham Palace.

name: Fusilier Museum London
description: The history of the Royal Fusiliers, within the Tower of London.

name: Royal Hospital Chelsea Museum
description: The story of the Chelsea Pensioners and their Wren-designed home.

name: Polish Institute & Sikorski Museum
description: Poland's military and cultural heritage in exile, in South Kensington.

name: Royal Mews
description: The royal carriages, cars and horses, at Buckingham Palace.

name: The King's Gallery (Buckingham Palace)
description: Changing exhibitions of treasures from the Royal Collection.

name: Honourable Artillery Company Museum
description: Military collection of the UK's oldest regiment (by appointment).

name: Bentley Priory Museum
description: The Battle of Britain and RAF Fighter Command HQ, in Stanmore.

name: Battle of Britain Bunker
description: The underground operations room that controlled the Battle of Britain, in Uxbridge.

name: Bruce Castle Museum
description: Tottenham's local history museum in a 16th-century manor house.

name: Barnet Museum
description: Volunteer-run local history museum for the Barnet area.

name: Hackney Museum
description: The history and diverse communities of the London Borough of Hackney.

name: Museum of Croydon
description: Croydon's local history and culture, in the Croydon Clocktower.

name: Honeywood Museum
description: Local history of Sutton in a historic house beside Carshalton Ponds.

name: Little Holland House
description: The Arts and Crafts home of artist Frank Dickinson, in Carshalton.

name: Vestry House Museum
description: Local history of Waltham Forest in a former workhouse.

name: Valence House Museum
description: Barking and Dagenham's local history museum, in a manor house.

name: William Morris Gallery
description: The only public gallery devoted to designer William Morris, in his childhood home.

name: Forty Hall & Estate
description: A Jacobean manor house and estate in Enfield.

name: Museum of Enfield
description: Enfield's local history collection, at the Dugdale Centre.

name: Gunnersbury Park Museum
description: Local history of west London in a Rothschild mansion.

name: Wandsworth Museum
description: Wandsworth's local history collection (check current operator).

name: Kingston Museum
description: Local history of Kingston, including the Eadweard Muybridge collection.

name: Headstone Manor & Museum
description: Harrow's museum in a moated medieval manor house.

name: Redbridge Museum
description: The history of the London Borough of Redbridge, in Ilford.

name: Museum of Wimbledon
description: Volunteer-run local history museum for the Wimbledon area.

name: Crystal Palace Museum
description: Tells the story of the Crystal Palace and its park.

name: Wandle Industrial Museum
description: The industrial heritage of the River Wandle, in Mitcham.

name: Twickenham Museum
description: Local history of Twickenham, Whitton and the Hamptons.

name: Wimbledon Lawn Tennis Museum
description: The history of tennis and the Championships, at the All England Club.

name: World Rugby Museum
description: The history of rugby union, at Twickenham Stadium.

name: MCC Museum (Lord's)
description: Cricket history at Lord's, home of the Ashes urn.

name: Arsenal Football Club Museum
description: The history of Arsenal FC, at the Emirates Stadium.

name: Chelsea FC Museum
description: The history of Chelsea FC, at Stamford Bridge.

name: Wimbledon Windmill Museum
description: The history of windmills and milling, in a working mill on Wimbledon Common.

name: Royal Academy of Arts
description: Britain’s first art school and gallery, founded 1768, famed for its Summer Exhibition.

name: Saatchi Gallery
address: Duke of York's HQ, King's Rd, Chelsea
description: Contemporary art gallery in Chelsea showcasing emerging and international artists.

name: Whitechapel Gallery
description: Influential East End gallery for modern and contemporary art since 1901.

name: Serpentine Galleries
description: Two contemporary art galleries in Kensington Gardens, home of the annual Serpentine Pavilion.

name: Hayward Gallery
description: Brutalist contemporary art gallery at the Southbank Centre.

name: Institute of Contemporary Arts (ICA)
description: Avant-garde arts centre on The Mall presenting art, film and performance.

name: Barbican Art Gallery
description: Contemporary art, design and architecture at the Barbican Centre.

name: The Photographers' Gallery
description: London's leading gallery dedicated to photography.

name: Camden Art Centre
description: Contemporary art gallery and learning centre in a Victorian building in Hampstead.

name: Newport Street Gallery
description: Damien Hirst's gallery showing works from his personal collection.

name: Two Temple Place
description: Opulent neo-Gothic mansion hosting a free exhibition each winter.

name: London Mithraeum Bloomberg SPACE
description: The reconstructed Roman Temple of Mithras, displayed in situ beneath Bloomberg’s HQ.

name: The Charterhouse
description: Historic almshouse and former monastery with a free museum and guided tours.

name: Banqueting House
description: The sole surviving part of the Palace of Whitehall, with a Rubens ceiling.

name: Tower Bridge Exhibition
description: High-level walkways with a glass floor and the Victorian engine rooms inside the bridge.

name: Golden Hinde
description: A full-size reconstruction of Sir Francis Drake's Tudor galleon.

name: Migration Museum
description: Explores how the movement of people to and from Britain has shaped the nation.

name: Burgh House & Hampstead Museum
description: A Queen Anne house with a local history museum and art gallery in Hampstead.

name: Bethlem Museum of the Mind
description: The history of mental healthcare and art by patients, at Bethlem Royal Hospital.

name: Alexander Fleming Laboratory Museum
description: The restored laboratory where Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928.

name: Heath Robinson Museum
description: Dedicated to illustrator William Heath Robinson and his ingenious contraptions.

name: Ben Uri Gallery and Museum
description: Focuses on the art and lives of Jewish, refugee and immigrant artists in Britain.

name: Dorich House Museum
description: The modernist home and studio of sculptor Dora Gordine, run by Kingston University.

name: Emery Walker's House
description: The best-preserved Arts and Crafts domestic interior in Britain, by the Thames.

name: All Hallows by the Tower Crypt Museum
description: Crypt museum of London's oldest church, with Roman pavement and Saxon remains.

name: Ragged School Museum
description: A recreated Victorian classroom in the East End school founded by Dr Barnardo.

name: Museum of the Order of St John
description: The history of the order behind St John Ambulance, in a Tudor gatehouse.

name: Kirkaldy Testing Museum
description: A Victorian materials-testing works with a giant working testing machine.

name: Charlton House
description: The finest surviving Jacobean mansion in London.

name: Boston Manor House
description: A restored Jacobean manor house in Brentford.

name: Hall Place & Gardens
description: A Tudor house with award-winning gardens beside the River Cray.

name: Eel Pie Island Museum
description: The music, boatbuilding and community history of Eel Pie Island.

name: Brent Museum and Archives
description: The local history of the London Borough of Brent.
